marketState: neutral, mixed signals with a bearish near-term tilt
summary: Front price ($3.22) is 'slightly over' modeled fair value ($2.93 now, band $2.43-$3.43), but that premium narrows over the next three weeks as fair value itself grinds up toward $3.06 and the next contract already reads FAIR. Seasonality gives a mild bullish tilt over 4 weeks (63% historical win rate, +2.3% typical move, Sep the strongest month) but reliability is explicitly flagged LOW (51pp spread), so it can't be leaned on hard. Weather shows a fading heat pulse: 1-7D demand is +8.3% vs the 5y average, but demand just dropped -27.6 in 24h and the 8-16D outlook has swung to -4% vs 5y, consistent with bearish daily/weekly technicals (50<200 EMA cross on both). Storage stays oversupplied (+175 Bcf vs 5y) and this week's build beat forecast by 13 Bcf (bearish surprise), while managed money is already net short at just the 31st percentile - a squeeze risk that argues against piling into fresh shorts. Net read: fundamentals and technicals roughly offset longer term, but the short-term setup favors fading the cooling heat spike back toward fair value.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality favors modest upside into September (63% 4-week win rate, +2.3% typical move) and managed money is net short at only the 31st percentile, leaving room to cover, but seasonality reliability is explicitly LOW (51pp 10-90% spread) and storage is +175 Bcf oversupplied with a bearish build surprise - the tailwinds and headwinds roughly cancel out.
longTerm.invalidation: A clean break and hold above $3.43 (top of the current fair-value band) alongside a narrowing storage surplus would favor chasing length; a break below $2.75 with the storage surplus widening further would favor a swing short instead.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for a pullback into the $2.95-$3.05 fair-value zone with a below-forecast storage build (surprise turning neutral/bullish) or the MM net-short percentile easing above 45 before adding length into peak/September seasonal strength.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D heat demand (+8.3% vs 5y) is fading fast (24h demand change -27.6, and 8-16D has swung to -4% vs 5y), while daily/weekly technicals remain bearish (50<200 EMA cross) and the front is already priced 'slightly over' fair value with the futures curve showing front > Sep > Oct (mild backwardation likely to correct as the heat spike passes).
shortTerm.invalidation: A renewed spike in 1-7D or 8-16D heat forecasts (demand pctVs5y turning sharply positive again), or price breaking and holding above $3.36-$3.43, would invalidate the short.

Technicals bearish on daily/weekly (50<200 EMA cross) despite neutral RSI readings
Seasonality mildly bullish over 4wk (63% win rate) but reliability flagged LOW; MM net short at 31st percentile (squeeze risk)
